editorNotes: Lugia scores well in simulations thanks to its incredible stats and neutral matchup profile. However, its low offensive power and its struggles against Zacian and other Steel types reduce its effectiveness in the metagame.

Lugia scores well in simulations thanks to its incredible stats and neutral matchup profile. However, its low offensive power and its struggles against Zacian and other Steel types reduce its effectiveness in the metagame.

Ho-oh is a vital anti-meta presence to combat Zacian and other Steel or Fairy types. Its high-energy attacks can threaten most Pokemon, but some may find its playstyle to be too cumbersome. It also faces significant challenges against Palkia.

Solgaleo's Steel typing and unique offenses give it an intriguing place in the metagame. However, its lack of closing power is a serious hindrance, and it's more likely to consume more shields than it earns. It can work as an anti-meta pick and on alignment heavy teams, but it lends a difficult win-more playstyle.

Primarina's stats may be eclipsed by its Legendary opponents, but it serves a vital and accessible anti-meta role against Palkia. Primarina depends on shields for success, so look to pair it with stat monsters who can absorb hits on its behalf.
